preamble: T-glottalization is one of the most widespread features of modern English dialects, where the alveolar stop [t] is replaced by a glottal stop [ʔ] in specific phonological environments.

examples.note: In Yucatec Maya, glottalized stops /pʼ tʼ kʼ/ followed by an identical consonant optionally debuccalize to [ʔ], losing their oral place of articulation. The ejective quality is retained as a pure glottal closure.
Word-final stop debuccalization /T/→[h] at phrase boundaries is well-documented in Yucatec. Final oral stops weaken to laryngeal segments in coda position across all places of articulation.
